Name: Coast-Warden of the Danes
Effect: On enter: Draw 'Beowulf, Son of Ecgtheow' from your deck.
Anecdote: 'What are you, armour-bearing men in mail-coats, who come sailing the sea-road? Never have shield-bearers landed here more openly.'

Name: Scop of Heorot
Effect: On enter: Draw a card. If 'Heorot, the Mead Hall' is in play, draw two cards instead.
Anecdote: 'Clear sang the scop in Heorot, of the beginning of men — and out in the darkness, the song was torment to the one who waited.'

Name: Danish Thanes
Anecdote: 'They slept in the hall after the beer-feast and knew no sorrow. They did not know how many of them would see morning.'

Name: Unferth, the Envious
Effect: While on top: Enemy heroes at this location have -1 Power.
Anecdote: 'Are you the Beowulf who lost a swimming-match to Breca? He begrudged any man a glory greater than his own.'

Name: Heorot, the Mead Hall
Effect: While on top: At the end of your turn, if you have a hero here, draw a card.
Anecdote: 'The greatest of halls, gold-adorned and wide-gabled. Its light shone over many lands.'

Name: Wealhtheow, Hostess of the Hall
Effect: On enter: Revive a human with a cost of [2] or less.
Anecdote: 'Gold-adorned she went among the men, offered the cup first to the king, then carried it to every warrior, old and young.'

Name: Wiglaf, the Loyal
Effect: On enter: If you have another hero here, draw two cards.
Anecdote: 'Not one of the chosen companions stood by him in the fire — only Wiglaf. Kinship can never be set aside in a man who thinks rightly.'

Name: Grendel
Effect: Cannot be played at a location where you have heroes. Defeated when you have one or more heroes here. Reward: Draw 'Grendel's Mother' from your deck and draw a card.
Anecdote: 'The grim spirit was called Grendel, walker of the borderlands. Thirty thanes he seized in their sleep and carried to his lair, exulting.'

Name: Hrothgar, King of the Danes
Effect: On enter: Draw a card for each monster in your underworld.
Anecdote: 'Twelve winters the lord of the Scyldings suffered. No reward he promised could buy a man to face the fiend — until the Geat came.'

Name: Breca, the Swimmer
Effect: While on top: Once per turn, you may move Breca to another location.
Anecdote: 'Five nights we swam together in the winter sea, swords bare in our hands against the whale-fishes.'

Name: Grendel's Mother
Effect: Cannot be played at a location where you have heroes. Defeated when you have two or more heroes here. Reward: Revive a being with a cost of [3] or less.
Anecdote: 'A hundred half-years she ruled the cold currents, the mere-wife, mighty in her grief and greedy for vengeance.'

Name: Beowulf, Son of Ecgtheow
Effect: Beowulf counts as two heroes for defeating monsters.
Anecdote: 'He had in his hand-grip the strength of thirty men. I scorn to bear a sword against this fiend, he said — and grappled bare-handed.'

Name: Geatish Shield-Companions
Anecdote: 'Fourteen of the keenest he chose, and the fifteenth was himself. The ship went out over the wave-way, most like a bird.'

Name: Hygelac, King of the Geats
Anecdote: 'He gave Beowulf seven thousand hides of land, a hall and a throne. To the brave, the ring-giver is never poor.'

Name: The Fire-Dragon
Effect: Cannot be played at a location where you have heroes. Defeated when you have two or more heroes here. Reward: You may play a card with a cost of [5] or less for free. Then banish your strongest hero here.
Anecdote: 'Three hundred winters it held the hoard in the earth, until a thief woke its wrath. It began to spew flame — and that was the beginning of the end.'
